name: Mustard~ German-Style
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine mustard seed and dry mustard.
2. In a 1- to 2-quart non-aluminum pan, combine remaining ingredients.
3. Simmer uncovered on medium heat until reduced by half, about 10-15 minutes.
4. Pour the mixture into the mustard mixture.
5. Let mixture soak at room temperature for 24 to 48 hours, adding additional vinegar if necessary to maintain enough liquid to cover seeds.
6. Process the seeds and mixture in a blender or food processor until pureed to the desired texture (at least 3 or 4 minutes). Some prefer whole seeds remaining, while others prefer a smooth paste.
7. The mixture will thicken considerably upon standing. If it gets too thick after a few days, stir in additional vinegar.
8. Scrape mustard into clean, dry jars; cover tightly and age for at least 3 days before using.
